note: Initial snapshot seeded from web-search-verified articles (RSS egress is blocked in the build sandbox). scripts/aggregate-live-feed.mjs replaces this with live RSS data on the first networked build.

items.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
items.summary: Midjourney is seeking to compel Disney, Universal, and Warner Bros. to disclose how they use AI internally, as part of its fair-use defense in the studios' copyright suits over its image models.

items.title: How America's 250th birthday became a test of AI-powered collective intelligence
items.summary: Specialized AI agents connected groups in real time for large-scale civic discussion around the US semiquincentennial — an experiment in 'hyper-communication' at national scale.

items.title: Trunk Tools' stack cut document review from 60 days to 10 by ditching general-purpose models
items.summary: A specialized three-layer architecture for construction-document automation shrank review cycles from months to days — a case study in domain models beating general-purpose LLMs.

items.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
items.summary: Microsoft Frontier Company is a new operating business focused on enterprise AI deployments, backed by $2.5 billion and 6,000 industry and engineering experts.

items.title: The latest AI news we announced in June 2026
items.summary: Google's June roundup: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate speech-to-speech across 70+ languages, a Gemini-built smart speaker, and Android 17 AI features.

items.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
items.summary: OpenAI began a limited preview of the GPT-5.6 series — Sol (flagship), Terra, and Luna — advancing coding, computer use, scientific research, and cybersecurity, with GA planned in the coming weeks.

items.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
items.summary: Jalapeño is OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator — a blank-slate inference design co-developed with Broadcom from design to tape-out in about nine months, targeting deployment by end of 2026.

items.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
items.summary: ChatGPT and Codex roll out to all Samsung Electronics employees in Korea and its worldwide DX division — one of OpenAI's largest enterprise deployments to date.

items.title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
items.summary: A Daybreak program run with Trail of Bits that applies GPT-5.5-Cyber to finding and patching vulnerabilities in critical open-source projects.

items.title: Introducing the FFASR Leaderboard: Benchmarking ASR in the Real World
items.summary: A new far-field automatic speech recognition benchmark and leaderboard measuring ASR systems under real-world acoustic conditions.

items.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
items.summary: TechCrunch's running tally of 2026 tech layoffs where companies explicitly cited AI as a factor — a live index of AI's labor-market impact.

items.title: Hugging Face on JFrog Artifactory: An Enterprise Guide (and What Changes in June 2026)
items.summary: How enterprises mirror and govern Hugging Face models through JFrog Artifactory, and what changed in the June 2026 integration.

items.title: Google I/O 2026: Sundar Pichai's opening keynote
items.summary: Pichai's I/O 2026 keynote: Gemini Omni, Gemini 3.5 Flash as the new AI Mode default, Gemini Spark, and the road to Gemini 3.5 Pro.

items.title: 85% of IT teams claim every AI agent is under control. Only 42% actually know who owns them.
items.summary: New research on agent sprawl: most IT teams assert every deployed AI agent has a named owner, but fewer than half say ownership is actually clear.

items.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
items.summary: Hugging Face's periodic survey of the open-source model ecosystem: release velocity, license trends, and the open-weights leaderboard landscape.
